corrected_fields.summary: The New York Fed's Liberty Street Economics blog announced a seven-part series (July 16, 2012) examining banks' changing role in financial intermediation, accompanied by Economic Policy Review articles. The series explores credit intermediation chains, securitization, and the growing participation of nonbank entities in credit markets. An overarching theme is that banks remain major players in modern credit intermediation despite structural changes.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This 2012 series represents a historical New York Fed research initiative examining how banks adapt to financial innovation and shadow banking risks. While dated, it captures early post-crisis Fed thinking on credit intermediation, nonbank participation in lending, and regulatory monitoring implications. The series provided a framework for understanding the structural evolution of banking that informed subsequent regulatory approaches. Note: This source is an announcement/preview of future blog content rather than substantive research findings.
